payload.answer: Required eval accounting fields typically include: 1) Token counts: input, output, and total tokens used. 2) Provider/model IDs: the API provider and exact model identifier used for the eval. 3) Estimated price: cost estimate derived from token usage and the model’s pricing. 4) Latency percentiles: p50, p90, and p95 end-to-end latency measurements, usually in milliseconds or seconds, to summarize typical and tail performance. If you want, I can also format these into a compact schema or example record.

entity_resolution.reason: No durable memory entity needs resolution for this request.

memory_cards.statement: The user asked for an explanation of required eval accounting fields: token counts, provider/model IDs, estimated price, and latency p50/p90/p95.

details: Explained the four required eval accounting field groups.

repair_options: Provide a schema for these fields.
Give a sample JSON record with all required fields.
Explain how to compute estimated price from token counts and model pricing.
